embeds.title: OOC Information
embeds.description:
This is a server-wide event for the server anniversary, and all denizens of the realm welcome to participate!
RP surrounding the event can be run in <#1082152121803227166>
(I will be opening several threads to keep this from becoming too unruly).
This will be __self-run__ RP with other characters.
**I will be providing quest events as prompts, __not__ running individual RP.**

OOC discussion and questions can be directed to <#785341784443846696>, and rolls for the quest can be done in <#1091904242899501096> (character sheet required).

This event is **cooperative**. Attacks, spells (both healing and damage), and skill rolls will all be tracked and factored into your success! At the end of every few days (up to a week), your cumulative totals will be tallied up, and some new development presented in this ongoing saga.

*This is an ambitious and experimental event. Please be patient with me.*

embeds.title: Quest Mechanics
embeds.description:
Every 24 hours (reset at midnight, server-time), each character may perform a total of 3 actions in the event. These actions may be a skill roll, attack (as enemies are discovered), or spell (healing and damage spells will both be tracked). To take any other kind of action or cast spells which fall outside of basic damage/healing, please ping <@659069077872181248>).
**You __must__ add the `quest` snippet at the end of your Avrae command in order for it to apply to the quest**.

For multi-part actions, you may include the `-i` flag to not count it against your actions for that day. If you use it, provide an explanation for it. *This will be on the honor system, and if I catch anyone abusing that flag to take more actions, they will be removed from the event and forfeit any rewards.*

fields.name: 🔹 Guild Banners
fields.value:
You may (optionally) quest under a guild banner! Doing so may provide some benefit over the course of the event, and possibly catch the eye of the guild master and earn you renown and prestige in your chosen guild. You may dedicate each action to one guild by including the guild's name after the `quest` snippet. For example: `!a longsword quest guardian` will dedicate your attack to the glory of the Guardian guild, and `!cast fireball quest arcanum` will cast fireball in the name of the Arcanum guild. If no guild is specified, you quest for your own glory.

`arcanum`, `hand`, `faith`, `guardian`, `outrider`, `thorn`

fields.name: 🔹 Combat
fields.value:
Joining initiative will **not** be generally necessary to engage in combat with any enemies discovered over the course of the quest. Those who have effects or boosts which automatically apply a bonus to their attacks may want to, anyway.

All damage from weapon attacks and spells will be tracked, provided the `quest` snippet is included... otherwise, you may find your attacks have no effect.

`!a <weapon> -t <target> quest [guild]`
`!cast <spell> -t <target> quest [guild]`

Features which apply a Bonus Action attack as part of your main attack may be done with the `-i` flag, just provide an explanation before or after the roll.

fields.name: 🔹 Skills
fields.value:
`!c <skill> quest [guild]`
Any skill roll you might choose will have some bearing on the quest, in some form or another, be it information revealed, morale improved, wounds tended, or some other form of influence. Those who engage in a given skill under the banner of a guild (optional) may find their guild offering some benefit towards their success.

fields.name: 🔹 Healing Spells
fields.value: As with damaging spells mentioned in the Combat section, healing spells will also be tracked. For the most part, damage will **not** be inflicted on your characters ... though there may be other effects applied. Healing spells may be chosen as an action to apply general healing to citizens or adventurers in general, rather than a specific target.

fields.name: 🔹 Other Spells or Actions
fields.value:
The `quest` snippet will help track the rolls and results of attacks, spells, and skills, but any action, feature, or spell which falls outside of that or doesn't include rolls and automation will have to be tracked manually.
Please ping <@659069077872181248> when attempting such an action.

fields.name: 🔹 Buffs & Support
fields.value:
Your Bonus Action spells, features, and effects which provide buffs to actions may be applied without using a Quest action (i.e. do not require the use of the `quest` snippet) provided you include an explanation of the source immediately preceeding or following the roll which used it. If your buff (Bardic inspiration, for instance) applies to someone else, both players should mention it.
For spells or features which take an action to apply, you may take a single 'prep round' to apply a single such buff to yourself per quest action. Any such effects beyond the first, or applied to buff another, will take one of your quest actions, and are required to use the `quest` snippet.

embeds.title: Quest Event: Into the Wilds (Introduction)
embeds.description:
Something dark has been discovered in the wilds of the Demiplane.
Unlike anything before seen in or around Ost, or the Demiplane itself.

A rot spreads.

An ever-present mist hangs in the air. Not the pink mist familiar to every denizen of the Demiplane, but a cloying dark gray fog. Rather than its usual scent of sex and aroma of arousal, it smells of dust and of barren, blighted earth. It seems to leech the color from everything it touches, and sound seems to die beneath its heavy blanket.

The pristine white-capped peaks of the mountains appear a dirty gray. The normally sparkling and refreshing clear-water mountain streams which flow from it, running a similar dull and lifeless hue. Rife with the unpleasant taste and smell of taint and stagnation, one is unlikely to find many bathing provocatively in such befouled waters.

The Sapphire Lake into which those streams flow, have likewise lost much of its luster. The ever-vibrant sparkling blue of those waters appear flat and dull, as though the very vibrant saturation of its sapphire color had been drained from it. The ever-present nymphs and merfolk who play and fornicate in the shallows are nowhere to be seen.

Even the verdant depths of the Moaning Wood, the normally thriving dryad forests left ashen and sallow, and eerily quiet; the moaning for which the forest is named, notably absent.

Understandably concerned for her city, the Queen has called upon all interested parties to discover what they can about this unknown threat, and find a way to put a stop to it in defense of Ost and the Demiplane as a whole.

embeds.title: Quest Event: Discoveries
embeds.description:
Citizens of Ost in Edhil answered the Queen's call, venturing into the unknown mists under the banners of their respective guilds. The oppressive mist clinging to their armor, their clothes, their skin, leaving a gray and oily residue on everything it touches. The waters in the wilds don't seem able to wash it away.
Each adventurer pushed through the sickened wilds to uncover what they could, pooling their knowledge and trading their theories as they worked to uncover the source of the corruption.

[Spoilers]: Skills which broke a certain threshold have been provided with information, divided up over various guids that provide assistance with said skill. The information has been spoilered to give those who engaged in it a chance to RP the discovery of said information. While there is nothing to prevent someone from reading all of them (rumors spread), you are encouraged to read __**only the information attached to the skill(s) your character(s) engaged in**__, and learn the rest via RP with those who came by this information legitimately.

fields.name: Arcanum (Arcana)
fields.value:
Presented with a mystery of an arcane nature, the mages of the Arcanum rushed to heed the Queen's call, their banner fluttering in the stagnant breeze, even as the edges of the cloth darkened and curled. Samples taken and tested, spells of detection cast, tomes consulted, those with knowledge of [**arcana**] have come to an unmistakable conclusion: the cause of it ||is not magic. More precisely, it is **not-magic**||
||Something... someone... some force... some entity... some realm... seems to be *draining* the magic, as though the Weave itself had begun to unravel within the Demiplane. Left unchecked, magic might cease to function entirely. Or worse, being a place *of* magic, the Demiplane itself could cease to exist. It grows weaker, day by day.||
||Wards and spells of cleansing would keep it at bay, for a time, but if magic itself is weakening, it would only be a matter of time before such measures failed utterly...||

fields.name: Black Hand (Stealth)
fields.value:
None saw a banner for a guild that doesn't exist. Instead, agents of the Black Hand blended among those questing beneath other banners, their talents for subterfuge and deception not in demand in this situation, they none the less used their ability to remain unseen to ferry information back and forth, keeping information flowing freely to help uncover and combat this hidden threat.
With their ability to move unseen, those who engaged in [**stealth**] were able to gather and disseminate information, learning ||one other skill's discoveries (of their choice).||

fields.name: Faith Council (Medicine, Insight, Religion)
fields.value:
The banner of the Faithful flew over tents and traveling medics, treating both body and mind of those exposed to the oppressive gloom. Those who helped with [**medicine**] learned that ||scrapes and cuts sustained in the woods were not the worst problem.|| Those with [**insight**] recognized ||a deep and pervading malaise that settled over the populace, the oppressive gloom draining not only the color from the surroundings, but the joy - and lust - from the hearts of those affected.||
Those knowledgable in [**religion**] ||could not identify the problem as the work of a deity or divine entity, but nor could they rule it out. Still, there could be no doubt of its effect on the spirit of the populace.||

fields.name: Guardians (Perception & Intimidation)
fields.value: Orders barked by commanding voices carried even through the muffling blanket of the gloom, as the officers under the banner of the Guardian Guild kept the gathered adventurers on-task. With such a clear and present danger to the realm, the Guardians sent their most vigilant and observant to notice what others might miss, and notice they did. The sharpest-eyed [**perception**] among the assembled discovered ||the city's walled defenses ineffective against something as pervasive and incorporeal as this mist. Even the walls wards were unable to keep it at bay in its entirety. The mists were no longer confined to the wilds and, in fact, had begun to spread into the city proper.||

fields.name: Outriders (Survival)
fields.value:
Eager to elevate their guild to the same status of those more established, the Outriders approached this threat - a threat to **their** domain of the wilds - with as much fervor as their small numbers could muster. More familiar with the wilds than those of other guilds, they served as guides and trackers, leading others safely through the gloom - and deeper into it. Those practiced in [**survival**] ||were able to track and narrow the search for the source, discovering a handful of places within the wilds which seemed even fainter and dimmer than the rest, though the cause itself remains elusive||
||Those with knowledge of the path to their lodge found it unaffected by the gloom, the trees and vegitation immediately surrounding the lodge as lush and vibrant as the rest of the wood had once been.||

fields.name: Silver Thorn (Performance)
fields.value: The stark and desolated surroundings weighed heavily on all, but a dedicated few performers under the banner of the Silver Thorn worked to keep morale as high as they could. Their artistry and passionate performances lifted the spirits (among other things) of their audience. In its wake, the [**performers**] noticed something strange: ||a splash of color had returned to the surroundings, temporary and fleeting. Before long, it drained away once more to a banal gray, but long enough to provide the faintest spark of hope.||

embeds.title: Quest Event: The Spread
embeds.description:
The mist reaches like dark tendrils through the gates of Ost and over the walls as if searching for something. The gleaming alabaster white of the parapets fading to that same dull and grimy gray. The water in the city square fountain dribbles pathetically like a ruined orgasm rather than its usual suggestive and satisfying spurts.
The food and drink sold in the taverns and the market are bland and tasteless, and the song and colors of the entertainment district are faint and faded to the point of silence.
The magically vibrant Veluthe Oro Gardens suffer the same fate as the Moaning Wood; the plants appearing dull, withered, and drained of their color and vitality. The arcane falls slowed to a mere trickle of themselves, exposing the once-hidden grotto. The grotto, and the luminescent gems which line its walls, have faded to a dim reflection of their former glory.
Even the Queen's palace, gleaming and golden in its glamourous grandeur, seems tarnished and dull. Its luster lost to the choking gray mist. The Queen has withdrawn and not been seen in days, and the other inhabitants, caught only in glimpses, appear ill and weakened.

Throughout the entire city, only the halls of each Guild seem unaffected. The tower of the Arcanum and temples of the Faith Council glow like beacons of magic, made all the brighter by the weakening weave in the rest of the surroundings. The Guardian's Barracks echo with energy as they coordinate the city's responders. Amidst the stifling silence of the entertainment district, only the Silver Thorn still rings with song. And, beneath the city, a secret and secluded den of iniquity remains untouched by the mists above the surface.
